Home / Services / Tile Flooring Installation

Tile Flooring Installation

Durable and stylish tile flooring options for commercial kitchens, restrooms, and more.

Tile flooring is an excellent choice for commercial spaces that require durability, water resistance, and easy maintenance. Commercial Flooring Installations specializes in the installation of ceramic tile, porcelain tile, and natural stone tile. We understand the unique challenges of installing tile in a commercial setting, such as heavy foot traffic, exposure to moisture, and the need for a slip-resistant surface.

Our experienced installers take great care in preparing the subfloor to ensure a level and stable base for the tile. We use only the highest-quality mortars and grouts to ensure a secure and long-lasting installation. We also offer a variety of tile patterns and designs to match your specific aesthetic preferences.

Whether you need tile flooring for a commercial kitchen, a restroom, or a retail store, we have the expertise and resources to deliver exceptional results. We work closely with our clients to select the right tile for their specific needs and budget. We also offer ongoing maintenance and repair services to keep your tile flooring looking its best for years to come.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Subfloor preparation and leveling
  2. 02 Step 2: Layout and precise cutting of tiles
  3. 03 Step 3: Professional installation using mortar
  4. 04 Step 4: Grouting and sealing for a water-resistant and durable finish

Benefits

  • Exceptional durability and water resistance
  • Easy to clean and maintain
  • Wide range of styles and designs
  • Slip-resistant options available

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the difference between ceramic and porcelain tile?

Porcelain tile is denser and more water-resistant than ceramic tile, making it a better choice for high-moisture areas.

How often should tile grout be sealed?

Tile grout should be sealed every 1-2 years to prevent staining and water damage.

What is the best way to clean tile floors?

Use a pH-neutral cleaner and a damp mop to clean tile floors. Avoid using abrasive cleaners or scouring pads.
